sections.title: 第 51 页
sections.text:
Get ready
Think What do living things have in common?
Listen and say.

1. get sunlight
2. grow
3. change
4. get food and water
5. move

Look and complete.

Xiaotan and her dog are eating breakfast. Eating a good breakfast helps them __ fast. After they get some __ and __ they are going to run in the park. Then they will have a __. They can get some __ there. This keeps them healthy.

sections.title: 第 52 页
sections.text:
**PREVIEW**
Read and write.

My toy bear is small and brown. It does not eat or drink, It does not grow.

Living and non-living things

This baby bear lives in the forest. It is big and brown. It likes eating food.

**Toy bear**  **Baby bear**

Is it big or small?
What colour is it?
Does it eat or drink?
Does it grow?

**Answer the question before you read.**
Can you name some living and non-living things?

sections.title: 第 53 页
sections.text:
Explore 
Living and

We see many things in our world every day. All these things are living or non-living. How are they different?

Living things
A living thing can grow and change. A plant is a living thing. An animal is a living thing. You are a living thing! Living things need air, food, and water. People and animals can move. They can get food and water from different places. Plants cannot move by themselves. They make their food from sunlight, water, and air. What living things do you see every day?

sections.title: 第 54 页
sections.text:
**Non-living Things**

A non-living thing does not need air, food, or water. A toy is a non-living thing. A non-living thing does not grow, but it can change. Look at the shoes. How are they different?

A ball can move. Is it a living thing? No, it isn't. It cannot move by itself. It does not grow. Look around you. What non-living things do you see?

All living and non-living things in our world are important. We should take care of them.

How are living and non-living things different?

sections.title: 第 55 页
sections.text:
A Read, and then tick (√) or cross (X).

Living things
Non-living things
People and animals                  Plants

grow    √                                 X

change

need air, food and water

move by themselves

B Read again and talk.

What are living and non-living things in the text?

…. are living things.
…. are non-living things.

What living things do you see every day?

Think: Can you think of any other differences between living and non-living things?

sections.title: 第 56 页
sections.text:
Grommor in use
Listen and chant.

My doll and me
I can jump up and down. My doll can't run or walk.
I can dance all around. My doll can't sing or talk.
I can eat, and I can drink. My doll can't sleep or play.
I can talk, and I can think. It's a non-living thing.
I'm a living thing. But I love it anyway.

Learn
A living thing can grow and change.
A non-living thing does not need air, food, or water.
A non-living thing does not grow, but it can change.

Play a game.

It's a non-living thing. It's brown.
It doesn't grow, and it doesn't need food or water.
It can't move by itself, but it can change.

It's the ball.

sections.title: 第 57 页
sections.text:
**Communicate**

Listen and number, then say.
Things in the park

Sunflowers are living things.
They like sunlight, but they don't need a lot of water.

sections.title: 第 58 页
sections.text:
**Think and talk.**
**In the Science Club**

**Living things**    **Non-living things**

- grow     - do not grow
- need food and water    - do not need food or water

**What things are living things?**
A … is a living thing,
It needs … it …

**What things are non-living things?**
A … is a non-living thing.
It ...

**Draw and write.**

Is a living/non-living thing.
It needs/does not need
It can/cannot

sections.title: 第 59 页
sections.text:
**Extend**

**Living to non-living**

Some living things can become non-living things. Is the boy's straw hat from a living thing?

Wheat grows in fields.

Farmers cut the wheat.

It becomes straw. The sun dries the straw.

People can make a hat with the straw.

Straw begins as a living thing: wheat. People make it into a hat. The wheat becomes a non-living thing.

sections.title: 第 60 页
sections.text:
Grandma Lihos has a bamboo steamer. She makes boozl in it. How can we make a steamer?

Bamboo can grow very tall.

People cut down the bamboo.

People cut the bamboo into pieces.

People turn the bamboo pieces into a steamer.

Bamboo begins as a living thing. People make a steamer with the bamboo pieces. The bamboo becomes a non-living thing.

sections.title: 第 61 页
sections.text:
**Part 1**
- People make a hat with the straw.
- Wheat grows in fields.
- Farmers cut the wheat.
- The sun dries the straw.

**Part 2**
- People cut the bamboo into pieces.
- People cut down the bamboo.
- People turn the bamboo pieces into a steamer.
- Bamboo grows tall.

----

**Think**
What other non-living things come from living things?
How are they the same? How are they different?

**Think more about the Big Question**
What are living things?

**Living things**
- can grow and change.
- need air, food, and water.
- are different from non-living things.
